title: [cross-orch:skillos] ~/.claude retention manifest ratification — review flywheel-touching rules (handoff 2026-05-09T173000Z, PR #174)

did_gates.gate: AG1 (parent handoff): No retention rule deletes a path flywheel-loop needs preserved beyond its window
did_gates.evidence: Protected paths cover all flywheel-loop write surfaces (skills, commands, hooks, references, memory, projects/*/memory, CLAUDE.md, secret-ledgers); proposed v2 retention windows >= flywheel-loop's 30d gap-hunt window; v2 rule deltas explicitly preserve the safety posture

did_gates.gate: AG2 (parent handoff): Per-tick artifact retention windows match flywheel's substrate-integrity contract
did_gates.evidence: Gap-hunt-probe reads ~/.local/state/flywheel/*.jsonl (NOT under ~/.claude/); 14d retention on tool-results doesn't touch flywheel's audit chain

did_gates.gate: AG3 (parent handoff): Receipt-ledger format mutually parseable for cross-orch audit
did_gates.evidence: skillos.claude_state_pruner_receipt.v1 schema is jq-parseable; mission_anchor_hash per-row enables cross-orch verification; 16 fields all machine-readable

no_bead_reason: cross-orch ratification gate; next-actionable owner is skillos:1 (ship v2 manifest with rule changes); not flywheel:1 scope to author rule fixes per parent handoff manifest ownership statement

L61_ecosystem_touch.no_touch_reason: cross-orch ratification response written to .flywheel/handoffs/; manifest is skillos-owned per parent handoff; no flywheel doctrine, INCIDENTS, canonical L-rule, or skill surface touched

mission_rationale: Ratification response unblocks skillos:1's Phase 16-α-1 propagation arc. Without flywheel:1's response, skillos:1 can't install the launchd plist; without rule corrections, the plist would have been installed against patterns that don't match the live filesystem layout (operational-health risk: claims-success-without-effect class). Serves continuous-orchestrator-uptime by ensuring the cross-orch retention substrate actually does what it claims when it ships.
